dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HRES 1104 IH: Directing the Office of Congressional Conduct to establish standards of conduct related to mental capacity of members of the House of Representatives.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Perez submitted the following resolution; which was referred to the Committee on Ethics

form.official-title: Directing the Office of Congressional Conduct to establish standards of conduct related to mental capacity of members of the House of Representatives.

section.header: Standards of conduct and whistleblower portal relating to mental capacity of Members of the House of Representatives

subsection.header: Office of Congressional Conduct; standard of conduct relating to cognitive impairment

paragraph.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the adoption of this resolution, the Office of Congressional Conduct shall—

subparagraph.text: develop a standard for what constitutes conduct, relating to a Member who is unable to behave at all times in a manner that reflects creditably on the House, as required under clause 1 of rule XXIII of the Rules of the House of Representatives, because the Member suffers from a significant and irreversible cognitive impairment; and
submit a report on such standard to the Committee on Ethics.
the Office of Congressional Conduct may consult with relevant offices of Congress and outside experts in developing such standard; and

not later than 90 days after receiving the report under paragraph (1)(B), the Committee on Ethics shall develop and issue a standard described under paragraph (1)(A) or issue the standard contained in such report.

subsection.header: Committee on Ethics; guidance on mental capacity
subsection.text: The Committee on Ethics shall draft and make publicly available specific guidance for employees of the House to safely and confidentially make disclosures to relevant committees and legislative branch offices on the mental capacity of Members.
